theme_and_archetype.archetype: Archetype 4: Swiss & High-Contrast (adapted for technical hardware control)
theme_and_archetype.description: A premium prosumer networking control app. It feels like a command center. Objective, rigid, and functional. 95% of the UI is monochrome, color exists only to guide action and indicate status (e.g., active tunnels, bandwidth).
forbidden_patterns: Soft pastel colors in dark mode

rules.anti_default_rule: Inter is banned for headings. Use IBM Plex Sans for structure and IBM Plex Mono for technical data.
rules.pairing_archetype: Swiss Brutalist (Tech): One primary font family with radical weight contrast, paired with a Mono font for numbers.

rules: Never use pure #000000; #050505 gives a slightly more deliberate tech feel.
Accent colors must be highly saturated for maximum visibility against dark surfaces.
Hover/Active states should increase contrast, not decrease it.

layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Use Tailwind generic scale: p-2, p-4, p-6, p-8. Be generous with negative space.
layout_and_spacing.bento_grid_mobile: Mobile-first Control Room Grid: 1 column wide for main charts (Bandwidth), 2 columns for secondary metrics (CPU, RAM).
layout_and_spacing.surface_strategy: The 'Technical' Look: Grid Borders. Cards have flat backgrounds and 1px borders. NO soft shadows in dark mode.
layout_and_spacing.alignment: Left-align dense content and numbers. Center-align only splash screens or empty states.

components.cards: Flat background, rounded-xl, 1px subtle border. No shadows in dark mode.
components.buttons: Sharp or slightly rounded (rounded-md). High contrast. Primary buttons use accent color background with inverted text. Secondary buttons are transparent with 1px border.
components.toggles_switches: When Active, track uses Accent Primary (Cyan) or Status Success (Green). When Inactive, track is surface_elevated.
components.segmented_controls: Used for Smart Route / Full Route. Background surface_elevated, active item surface with 1px border.
components.charts: Line charts for bandwidth should use smooth bezier curves. Fill under the line with a subtle gradient of the line color (opacity 20% to 0%).
components.icons: Use lucide-react-native or @expo/vector-icons (Feather/Phosphor) for a clean, consistent technical look.

elements: Login view: Admin password input, prominent Biometric (FaceID/TouchID) button.
Bandwidth Card: Real-time download/upload with sparkline chart. Monospace fonts for Mbps.
Hardware Stats Grid: 2x2 grid of small cards for CPU%, RAM%, Temp, Uptime.
Quick Actions: 4 rectangular toggle buttons for DPN, AdBlock, Parental Controls.
Top: Segmented control for Mode (Smart Route / Full Route).
Active Tunnels: Card list. Each card shows Country Flag, Node IP, and Latency (Green <50ms, Yellow <150ms).
Add Tunnel Button: Dashed border card.
Reboot Device: Danger zone button at the bottom.
List item: Device Icon (laptop, phone), Name, IP (Mono), Tx/Rx traffic indicator.
Each row has a mini DPN toggle switch to bypass/route traffic.

images.description: Abstract tech network mesh, dark deep obsidian background, glowing electric cyan and signal amber nodes.
Modern sleek logo icon for a cybersecurity router, minimalist shield mixed with infinity loop.

motion_and_interactions.transitions: Fast, precise micro-interactions (0.15s ease-out). Hardware tools should feel instantaneous.
motion_and_interactions.toggles: Satisfying snap animation with color transition.

accessibility.test_ids: ALL interactive elements MUST have testID prop for React Native. Format: kebab-case, role-based. e.g. testID="dpn-toggle-switch"
accessibility.contrast: Ensure text over dark backgrounds meets minimum 4.5:1 ratio.

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement these guidelines in React Native code using .js files. Use standard React Native StyleSheet or NativeWind. Ensure all layouts are mobile-first and responsive. Integrate testID props everywhere. Provide a strict dark/light mode context or global style object based on the colors specified.
